input:
Poem:
The lighthouse is very tall and bright,
It shines its big light in the night.
Ships can see it so they don't crash,
The lighthouse is good, it's not trash.
It stands by the sea all day too,
The lighthouse is nice, this is true.

Please give me your honest comments and feedback on this poem.

input:
Poem:
The lighthouse stands above the bay,
its lantern turning, night and day.
It warns the ships of rocks below
and guides them safely as they go.
Alone it waits through wind and rain,
then lights the dark for them again.

Please give me your honest comments and feedback on this poem.

metadata.topic: A lighthouse keeping watch and guiding ships.
metadata.poem:

input:
Poem:
All night it spends its single coin of light,
flung out and gathered, flung out and withdrawn--
a slow pulse keeping vigil on the rocks.
The sea forgets each warning by the dawn;
the keeper doesn't. He has learned to give
to those who pass and never know his name.

Please give me your honest comments and feedback on this poem.
